summary: Hardened the repository hygiene just recipes so Linux Nix checks fail fast when required formatting and linting tools are missing, kept macOS compatibility with explicit non-Linux skips for missing Linux hygiene tools, moved SwiftFormat and SwiftLint into explicit macOS-native recipes under macos-check, and documented the Linux-required versus macOS-native tooling boundary.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux nix develop -c just check fails with a clear missing-tool message if required repo hygiene tools are unavailable.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: PATH=/run/current-system/sw/bin just require-tool taplo failed with exit code 127 and printed: required tool missing: taplo; run inside nix develop.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux nix develop -c just check runs TOML formatting, shell formatting, typos, markdownlint, actionlint, and line-limit checks without optional skips.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: nix develop -c just check passed and output showed require-tool plus direct execution for taplo, shfmt, typos, markdownlint-cli2, actionlint, and just lint-lines.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS-native SwiftFormat and SwiftLint behavior remains compatible with just macos-check.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: justfile now routes macos-check through macos-swiftformat and macos-swiftlint after the shared check recipe; missing Linux hygiene tools print explicit non-Linux skips on Darwin, while each Swift Darwin branch requires the native tool and runs the previous SwiftFormat/SwiftLint commands.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Documentation or evidence records the Linux-required and macOS-native tool boundary.
No product QUIC, decode, render, presentation, or latency claim is changed.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Only justfile, docs/ENGINEERING.md, and node evidence/report files were changed.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node only hardens local repository hygiene tooling. Linux Nix validation and a focused missing-tool check were run.
